sections_cited: section 56 of the Specific Relief Act

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI CP D 6350 of 2020 ___________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge(s) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on office objection. 2. For hearing of CMA No.27213/2020. 3. For hearing of main case. 03.03.2025 Miss Naheed Akhtar Bhatti, advocate for the petitioner. Ms. Naheed Akhter, advocate holding brief for Mr. Iqbal M. Khurram, advocate for the respondent. Mr. Sandeep Malani, Assistant Advocate General Sindh. This petition is pending since 2020. Primary prayer clause specifically seeks directions against official respondent to not exercise their official functions. Prima facie, this offends section 56 of the Specific Relief Act. Latter prayer clause asserts proprietary rights in the immovable property, however, adjudication of the same is not amenable in writ jurisdiction. Learned counsel states that even though it is not mentioned in the prayer clause but the petitioner also apprehends harassment. Such plea also cannot be entertained in view of Division Bench judgment reported as PLD 2019 Sindh 168. In view of the foregoing, petition is found to be misconceived, hence, dismissed. Judge Judge Khuhro/PA
